author: trickycrypto
content:
I just want to add, statements like this make it seem like there is no desire to let the community decide, so why even hold a vote? If core devs want to have control over the decision then make that clear and don’t do any voting or community input.

Just be honest with us.

author: abcoathup
content:
I'm an Eth Magicians moderator so am able to access IP addresses.  I ran a script in the console whilst logged in to report on registration IPs shared by 2+ voters.  (I can share the script with any moderator to verify).

There were 3 pairs of accounts, where each pair was registered from the same IP address, on the same day and only voted Flamingo.  This strongly suggests duplicate voting.

There was a fourth pair of accounts with the same registration IP, but the accounts were created 6 months apart and only one account included a vote for Flamingo (amongst their max 3 votes).  This doesn't appear to be duplicate voting so was ignored.

Discounting all 3 pairs (6 votes) it is 39 🦩 to 39 🐻‍❄️ 
If we only discounted the duplicate 3 votes it is 42 🦩 to 39 🐻‍❄️ 

Discussing with <@350724220722085890> (Mascot Wrestler), I suggest we have a 2 week runoff, 🦩  vs 🐻‍❄️ , single choice vote.
Votes from accounts created after July 10 won't be counted.
Votes found to share a registration IP address and creation date won't be counted.
Requiring specific Discourse activity or trust levels would unfortunately exclude legitimate community voting.

author: trickycrypto
content: Thanks for clarifying ab. While I worry that since our community lives on a different platform, it will be hard for us to get the same voter turnout, I can only support this since the validity of the vote is important.

author: 0xunclebill
content: im just joking around man, i didnt realize this mascot thing was so contentious. LH doesnt usually do ascii art

author: trickycrypto
content:
Sorry for misunderstanding then, but to be clear, similar sentiments have been made sincerely by others. So it would seem people on both sides care. Sure, not enough to lose track of what’s more important, but at least on my end, I saw the request for community input and thought it’d be fun to brainstorm a fitting mascot and see what people think.

But it would seem I may have been wasting my time if some devs were going to veto or protest any options which weren’t their own anyway. At this point I think it’s more about the sour taste that leaves behind than what ASCII art gets printed in our CLIs later this year.

author: nero_eth
content: new vote for two weeks, polar bear vs flamingo, sound good!
